name: Art Blakey
abstract: Arthur Blakey (October 11, 1919 – October 16, 1990) was an American jazz drummer and bandleader. He was also known as Abdullah Ibn Buhaina after he converted to Islam for a short time in the late 1940s.

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: So I said, 'Yeah. I can come tomorrow.' ... He said to me, 'Can you join the group?' I said, 'Art, I’d love to,' ... That was the last I said. I was a Messenger and didn't know it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: DeFranco Buddy
about_quotes.evidence: I said to Art, “Lets make a group out of this and go out.” And Art said, “Yeah, lets do it.” So we did, we became a bona fide traveling group.
about_quotes.doc_title: DeFranco Buddy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Watson
about_quotes.evidence: I was with him four and a half years... I was at the age where I was like open to that... he could work with me, like putty in his hands.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Watson interviewed by Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, January 19, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Putter Smith
about_quotes.evidence: His collaborations on stage and in the studios included work with Thelonious Monk, Art Blakey, The Beach Boys, and Sonny & Cher.
about_quotes.doc_title: Putter Smith interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 27, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Bruce Johnstone
about_quotes.evidence: And so I would hear all these great things like Art Blakey, Horace Silver, Max Roach, all these wonderful players.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bruce Johnstone interviewed by Monk Rowe, Dunkirk, New York, October 11, 2013

about_quotes.speaker: David Weiss
about_quotes.evidence: Apparently in 1967 Art Blakey hired Slide Hampton for the Messengers and to be the musical director.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Weiss interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 3, 2022
